title: galarhoeus pekinensis subsp. barbellatus
intro: Galarhoeus pekinensis subsp. barbellatus, also known as the Chinese spurge, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. Galarhoeus pekinensis subsp. barbellatus can be prepared as a herbal tea or decoction for medicinal use, and it is also sometimes used topically in the form of a poultice or ointment to treat skin conditions.
